title: All repositories enabled in podman container when host has Simple Content Access enabled
body:
### Issue Description

The host is registered to Satellite which has SCA (Simple Content Access) enabled.

SCA/Subscriptions work fine (are finally usable) on the host after:
https://community.theforeman.org/t/rfc-making-things-easier-when-working-with-custom-products-simple-content-access-sca/31334
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2188380
https://projects.theforeman.org/issues/36301

But once inside a container all repos are enabled, causing (in our case) all package installs/updates to fail.

If not a bug, what is the recommended method of working around this?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have many repos (both RH and non-RH) in Satellite
2. Enable SCA, hosts get overrides automatically from "Auto-create content overrides when turning on SCA" feature.
3. dnf update inside a container


### Describe the results you received

Container, dnf repolist:
```
hakongi@ou-git-runner-01:~$ podman run -it lsource2.decode.is:5050/ito/containers/molecule:main /bin/sh
(app-root) (app-root) dnf repolist

Unable to read consumer identity
Subscription Manager is operating in container mode.
```

comments.body: I suggest you report this through the Red Hat support channels. Upstream doesn't explicitly configure any of this. The subscriptions mounts are configured via config file so it would be much better handled there.
